summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #744 where connecting to a wss:// URL in the node host code path caused a panic due to a missing rustls CryptoProvider, and write a regression test.
summary.outcome: Added rustls CryptoProvider installation at the start of NodeHost::run(), added the rustls dependency to node-host Cargo.toml, wrote a regression test, and committed the fix with all 16 tests passing and clean clippy/fmt.

repo: The gateway code path installs rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider() in gateway.rs:54, but node-host had no equivalent — the fix mirrors that pattern
tokio-tungstenite uses rustls for wss:// connections via feature unification in the final binary; node-host needed rustls-tls-webpki-roots feature enabled explicitly to be self-contained
The project uses 'beads' for internal issue tracking alongside GitHub issues
Cargo.toml dependencies are kept in alphabetical order by convention

code.finding: NodeHost::run() calls tokio_tungstenite::connect_async() on wss:// URLs but previously never installed a rustls CryptoProvider, causing a panic on Windows
Added rustls dependency and rustls-tls-webpki-roots feature on tokio-tungstenite

workflow: Using a fast-failing address (e.g. localhost with closed port) in network tests avoids 75s timeouts from real network connection attempts
The fix pattern for missing rustls CryptoProvider is: rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() — ignore the error since it may already be installed

friction: Initial regression test used a real wss:// address causing a 75-second timeout before the test passed — had to update to a fast-failing local address
